question_text: Suppose that X has the probability density function f(x) = Γ(α)xα−1e−λx if x > 0 otherwise, where α > 0 and λ > 0. Which one of the following statements is NOT true?

options.A: E(X) exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0
options.B: Variance of X exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0
options.C: E � 1  exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0
options.D: E(log(1 + X)) exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0

solution:
1) Understanding the probability density function:
This is the probability density function of a Gamma distribution with shape parameter α and
rate parameter λ. The mean and variance of a Gamma-distributed random variable are
well-defined for all α > 0 and λ > 0.
2) Analysis of the options:
(A) E(X) exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0:
This is true because the mean of a Gamma distribution exists for α > 0 and λ > 0.
(B) Variance of X exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0:
This is true because the variance of a Gamma distribution exists for α > 0 and λ > 0.
(C) E � 1
 exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0:
This is NOT true. For small values of X, 1
X becomes very large, and the expectation E � 1
does not exist for all values of α > 0 and λ > 0.
(D) E(log(1 + X)) exists for all α > 0 and λ > 0:
This is true. The logarithmic transformation of a Gamma distribution’s expectation exists for
all α > 0 and λ > 0.
The correct answer is (C).
Quick Tip
- The expectation of 1
X does not always exist for all values of α > 0 and λ > 0 due to
the behavior of X near 0.
- Expectation functions involving logarithms or reciprocals can have existence condi-
tions based on the tail behavior of the distribution.
